explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the user stops the ventilation while the system is in PCV mode (bool_exp2), then eventually (F) the system will transition to StandbyMode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff) (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the transition shall occur if the specified condition is met.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV mode and the user stops ventilation, then eventually the system will be in Standby Mode (VentilationOff). This captures the required transition from PCV to VentilationOff upon the user stopping ventilation.

The property states that globally, if the user stops the ventilation while the system is in PCV mode, then in the next state, the system will transition to Standby Mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode). This captures the immediate transition implied by 'The transition...shall occur'.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'PCVMode' and the user triggers 'stopVentilation', then in the very next state, the system must transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is synonymous with VentilationOff). This captures the immediate nature of the 'transition shall occur' as implied by the 'next' (X) operator.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in PCV Mode and the user stops the ventilation (trigger condition), then eventually the system must transition to Standby Mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff, the consequence). This directly translates the 'if P then eventually Q' pattern.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PCV mode (bool_exp1) and the user stops the ventilation (bool_exp2), then eventually the system transitions to Standby Mode (bool_exp3), which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode. This captures the required transition logic.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode (bool_exp1) and the user stops ventilation (bool_exp2), then in the next state (X), the system will transition to Standby Mode (bool_exp3), which represents VentilationOff. This accurately captures the required transition logic.

The LTL property ensures that whenever the user stops the ventilation while the system is in PCV mode, it will eventually transition to the Standby Mode, which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode. This is captured by a global implication where the antecedent is the combined condition (user stops ventilation AND in PCV mode) and the consequent is the eventual occurrence of Standby Mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV mode and the user stops the ventilation, then in the next state, the system must transition to Standby Mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode). This directly captures the requirement for a transition to occur upon the specified condition.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'PCVMode' and the user triggers 'stopVentilation', then in the very next state, the system must transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is synonymous with VentilationOff). This accurately captures the required transition condition and immediate effect.
